---
title: "Getting Started"
---

## Prerequisites

- [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/) or [OrbStack](https://orbstack.dev/)
- [Rust toolchain](https://rustup.rs/) (for building from source)

## Build the Sandbox Image

The sandbox image is what runs inside each container. It includes Python, Node.js, common CLI tools, and runs as a non-root user.

```bash
docker build -t den-sandbox:latest -f Dockerfile.sandbox .
```

The image includes:

| Tool | Version |
|------|---------|
| Ubuntu | 24.04 |
| Python | 3.12 + uv |
| Node.js | 22 LTS + pnpm, yarn |
| CLI tools | ripgrep, fd-find, jq, curl, wget |
| Build tools | build-essential, cmake, git |
| PDF tools | poppler-utils, qpdf, file |

## Build and Run Den

```bash
# Build
cargo build --release

# Run with default config
./target/release/den --config den.toml
```

Den will:

1. Connect to the Docker daemon
2. Clean up orphaned containers/volumes from previous runs
3. Pre-warm the container pool (default: 2 containers per tier)
4. Start listening on `0.0.0.0:8080`

## Override the Port

```bash
./target/release/den --config den.toml --port 9090
```

## Verify It's Running

```bash
curl localhost:8080/api/v1/health
# → ok
```

## Your First Sandbox

```bash
# Create
ID=$(curl -sf -X POST localhost:8080/api/v1/sandboxes \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"tier":"default"}' | jq -r .id)

echo "Sandbox: $ID"

# Run a command
curl -sf -X POST "localhost:8080/api/v1/sandboxes/$ID/exec" \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"cmd":["python3","-c","print(\"hello from sandbox\")"]}' | jq .

# Check resource usage
curl -sf "localhost:8080/api/v1/sandboxes/$ID/diagnostics" | jq .

# Clean up
curl -sf -X DELETE "localhost:8080/api/v1/sandboxes/$ID/volume"
```
